intro: Cyril of Alexandria (c. 376–444), patriarch of Alexandria, was the chief architect of orthodox Christology. At the Council of Ephesus (431) he led the condemnation of Nestorius and the affirmation of Mary as Theotokos (“God-bearer”), insisting on the unity of Christ’s person. A formidable exegete, his Commentary on Luke survives largely in an ancient Syriac version. Later tradition called him the “Seal of the Fathers.”
